author: śrīla bhaktisiddhānta sarasvatī ṭhākura
verses:
śrīmatī rādhikā⋅kṛṣṇakāntā⋅śiromaṇi
kṛṣṇapriya⋅madhye tā̐’ra sama nāhi dhanī

munigaṇa śāstre rādhā⋅kuṇḍera varṇane
gāndharvikā⋅tulya kuṇḍa karaye gaṇane

nāradādi⋅priyavarge je prema durlabha
anya sādhakete tāhā kabhu nā sulabha

kintu, rādhā⋅kuṇḍe snāna jei jana kare
madhura rasete tā̐’ra snāne siddhi dhare

aprākṛta⋅bhāve sadā jugala⋅sevana
rādhā⋅pāda⋅padma labhe sei harijana

śrī⋅vārṣabhānavī kabe dayitadāsere
kuṇḍa⋅tīre sthāna dibe nija⋅jana ka’re

‘upadeśāmṛta’ dhari’ rūpānuga⋅bhāve
jīvana jāpile kṛṣṇa⋅kṛpā sei pā’be

satya, tretā, dvāparera je⋅sakala bhakta
kṛṣṇakṛpā labhiyāche gṛhastha⋅virakta

bhāvī⋅kāle, vartamāne bhaktera samāja
sakalera padarajaḥ jāce dīna āja

bhakativinoda⋅prabhu anuga je⋅jana
dayita⋅dāsera tā’ra pade nivedana

dayā kari’, doṣa hari’, bala ‘hari! hari!’
‘upadeśāmṛta’⋅vāri śiropari dhari’
en_translation:
Śrīmatī Rādhikā is the crest-jewel of Kṛṣṇa's beloveds. Among Kṛṣṇa's beloveds, there is no one as rich in love as Her.

In the scriptures describing Rādhā-kuṇḍa, the sages count the Kuṇḍa as equal to Gāndharvikā (Rādhā).

The love that is rare even for the dear devotees like Nārada is never easily attainable by other practitioners.

But one who bathes in Rādhā-kuṇḍa attains perfection in that sweet mellow through his bath.

Serving the Divine Couple eternally in a transcendental mood, that devotee of Hari attains the lotus feet of Rādhā.

When will Śrī Vārṣabhānavī, accepting him as Her own, give Dayita-dāsa a place on the bank of the Kuṇḍa?

If one passes his life holding the 'Upadeśāmṛta' in the mood of a follower of Rūpa Gosvāmī, he will obtain Kṛṣṇa's mercy.

All the devotees of Satya, Tretā, and Dvāpara yugas who were detached householders attained Kṛṣṇa's mercy.

Today, this lowly person begs for the dust of the feet of all the devotees in the society of devotees, both in the future and the present.

Whoever is a follower of Bhaktivinoda Prabhu—Dayita-dāsa makes this submission at his feet.

Be merciful, remove my faults, and say "Hari! Hari!", holding the water of 'Upadeśāmṛta' upon my head.
